conditionDescriptions: Chronic kidney disease with eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73m²
contraindications.clinicalRationale: Metformin is excreted unchanged by the kidneys. When eGFR falls below 30 mL/min/1.73m², there is impaired drug excretion and risk of lactic acid accumulation, leading to potentially fatal lactic acidosis.
contraindications.riskDescription: Lactic acidosis - potentially fatal metabolic complication
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider insulin, DPP-4 inhibitors, or other glucose-lowering drugs suitable for severe CKD
contraindications.sourceSnippet: metformin should be discontinued when eGFR falls below 30 mL/min/1.73m² to reduce risk of lactic acidosis. eGFR <30: Stop metformin; do not initiate metformin

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

contraindications.clinicalRationale: No safety data available for metformin use in dialysis patients. Drug accumulation and lactic acidosis risk due to inability to clear metformin through dialysis.
contraindications.riskDescription: Lactic acidosis due to drug accumulation
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Insulin, DPP-4 inhibitors suitable for dialysis patients
contraindications.sourceSnippet: there are currently no safety data for metformin use in patients with an eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73m2 or in those who are on dialysis. dialysis initiation: discontinue metformin

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Dose reduction required when eGFR <45 mL/min/1.73m² and may be considered for some patients with eGFR 45-59 mL/min/1.73m²
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients with moderate to severe chronic kidney disease (eGFR 15-59 mL/min/1.73m²)
warnings.actionRequired: Reduce dose when eGFR <45 mL/min/1.73m²; initiate at half dose and titrate to half maximum when eGFR 30-44 mL/min/1.73m²

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Monitor eGFR at least annually in all patients, increase frequency to every 3-6 months when eGFR <60 mL/min/1.73m²
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor eGFR at least annually; increase monitoring frequency to every 3-6 months when eGFR drops below 60 mL/min/1.73m²

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Monitor for vitamin B12 deficiency in patients treated with metformin for more than 4 years
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients on long-term metformin therapy (>4 years) or at risk for B12 deficiency
warnings.actionRequired: Consider vitamin B12 level monitoring in patients on long-term metformin treatment (>4 years) or those at risk (malabsorption, vegans)

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Gastrointestinal adverse events occur in up to 25% of patients with immediate-release formulation, leading to discontinuation in 5-10%
warnings.actionRequired: Consider extended-release formulation if significant GI side effects occur with immediate-release; start with low dose and titrate gradually

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease
